title: carduus onopordioides subsp. atropatanicus
intro: Carduus onopordioides subsp. atropatanicus, also known as a type of thistle, is a perennial plant belonging to the Asteraceae family. It is traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins. The health benefits of Carduus onopordioides subsp. atropatanicus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system, making it a valuable herbal remedy for various ailments. The plant can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and extracts, which can be consumed orally or used topically to harness its medicinal properties.
